title: He Touched Me, So I Live To Know
content:
He touched me, so I live to know
    That such a day, permitted so,
    I groped upon his breast.
    It was a boundless place to me,
    And silenced, as the awful sea
    Puts minor streams to rest.

    And now, I'm different from before,
    As if I breathed superior air,
    Or brushed a royal gown;
    My feet, too, that had wandered so,
    My gypsy face transfigured now
    To tenderer renown.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "He Touched Me, So I Live To Know"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
